title: Frankincense and sweet orange soy jar candle
subtitle: A 200 ml container candle with a warm resinous base and a bright citrus top
excerpt: Frankincense essential oil gives this soy container candle a warm, woody-resinous base note; sweet orange lifts it with a bright, cheerful citrus top. The pairing is grounding and clean. A 200 ml glass jar with a CD-12 wick gives a 25 to 30-hour burn. Both oils are toxic to cats; keep the candle out of rooms where cats spend time.
sourceNotes: Soy wax container candle technique: Candlescience wick sizing guide (2023) for CD-12 in a 6 to 7 cm diameter jar. Fragrance load at 8% by wax weight: standard maximum for GW 464 soy wax. Frankincense EO (Boswellia carterii or B. sacra): resinous, slow-releasing base note. Sweet orange EO (Citrus sinensis, cold-pressed): bright citrus top note. Both are reported toxic to cats (limonene in sweet orange, furanocoumarins in frankincense); note in safety section.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 200 ml jar candle (130 g soy wax fill)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights by the number of jars. Keep one pre-tabbed wick per jar.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure for 3 to 5 days before burning for maximum fragrance throw.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Effectively indefinite for the wax. Fragrance fades after about 2 years. Keep the lid on between burns. Discard if the wax surface discolours or the wick base corrodes.

glossaryTerms.definition: The first burn of a candle sets the wax memory: the diameter of the melt pool on the first burn becomes the maximum diameter it will reach on all subsequent burns. Burning until the pool reaches the jar edge on the first burn prevents tunnelling.
The temperature at which liquid wax is poured into the container. Soy container wax is poured at 55 to 65°C; too hot and the wax pulls away from the sides; too cool and it sets unevenly.

content.text: Frankincense and sweet orange sit at opposite ends of the volatility spectrum. Frankincense is a slow, resinous base note that releases steadily through the burn; sweet orange is a bright top note that carries on the cold throw and in the first minutes of the burn. In a soy container candle, the combination gives a clean, grounding scent that is neither too heavy nor too sharp. This candle uses essential oils rather than fragrance oils; the cold throw is softer than a synthetic blend but the character of both oils comes through clearly. Both frankincense and sweet orange essential oils are toxic to cats; keep the candle out of rooms where cats are present.

items.prepNote: Container-blend soy wax; GW 464 or equivalent
3.8% of wax weight; resinous base note
4.2% of wax weight; bright citrus top note; toxic to cats
CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm diameter jar

content.text: Never melt wax over a direct flame. Place the soy wax in the candle pouring pitcher and set in a pan of barely simmering water. Heat, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully melted and reaches 80°C. Remove from the heat and allow to cool.

While the wax cools, secure the sustainer tab of the pre-tabbed wick to the centre of the jar base using a small dab of melted wax or a wick sticker. Thread the wick through the centring bar and rest the bar across the jar rim so the wick hangs plumb in the centre.
Add the essential oils and pour
When the wax has cooled to 60°C, add the frankincense and sweet orange essential oils and stir for 2 minutes so the oils are fully incorporated. Pour at
: between 55 and 65°C. Pour in a slow, steady stream down the side of the jar, leaving a 5 to 10 mm gap at the top. Leave undisturbed until fully set.
Leave the candle to cure for at least 1 day before burning; 3 to 5 days gives the best fragrance throw. Trim the wick to 5 mm before each burn.
. Burn the candle until the melt pool reaches the full diameter of the jar, which takes 3 to 4 hours for a 200 ml jar. Extinguish before the last 10 mm of wax. Do not burn in a draught.

Keep the candle with its lid on between burns to preserve the fragrance. In a cool, dark place the wax is stable for 5 years or more; the essential oil scent fades noticeably after around 2 years. Toxic to cats; do not burn in rooms where cats are present.
